Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

It would have been better to resubmit against the existing patch in http://review.whamcloud.com/165 instead of submitting a patch as a new change.  That would keep Lai as the original author of the patch, and would make it easier to compare the new patch against the old one.

I think this could be done in a much simpler manner using a "--bind" mount at the client.  Essentially:

- mount the filesystem in some temporary location
- mount --bind the subdirectory to the final mountpoint

That said, it is a bit messy to have a temporary mountpoint in the middle of mounting each subdirectory (e.g. in case of automount mounting a subdirectory for each user).  The benefit is that there is only a small amount of complexity inside mount.lustre instead of a larger amount of complexity in the Lustre kernel code.

For fid2path, it would be cleaner to fix this in the kernel instead of returning all the way to the "filesystem root" and then chopping off the "subdirectory" part of the path. The kernel could stop traversing the path upwards when it hits the vfsmount mnt_root dentry, or possibly the directory on which the ioctl is being run.  That would ensure that fid2path also works as expected if a subdirectory is mounted using --bind (whether done by mount.lustre or not) by some user or chroot or as the root filesystem of some client node.

Actually, I'll leave this at 0 and let Oleg decide whether it is better to land the current version of this patch, or hold out for a better implementation.

The good news is that this patch is purely a client-side change and there is no reason it cannot be improved at a later time.  The bad news is that usually the "improved version" never arrives because people will invariably get busy with something else more important than fixing something which mostly works.

in your Lustre git checkout.

does it introduce any _additional_ RPC to mount a subset?

Patch Set 27:

No, it just renames the existing MDS_GETSTATUS to be MDS_GETROOT, to better match what is actually done by that RPC.  All it has ever done is return the /ROOT FID to the client (i.e. fileset=""), and now this is extended to return the named fileset (currently only a subdirectory under /ROOT/{fileset}), but conceivably virtual filesets in the future (e.g. a flat namespace of FIDs or similar).

Patch Set 29:

The "lfs migrate -m" command needs to be blocked on the client by checking the migrate FID against the FID of the filesystem root inode, since it is the only one that cares about migrating the root directory. The MDS shouldn't have to track each client mountpoint separately, unless this patch is also going to track the mountpoint as an open file so that it cannot be unlinked on another client? 

That might be an interesting test to add - to mount a fileset in sanityn, and then rmdir the directory on the other mountpoint and try to access the fileset again. There are some existing tests that do this for regular directories, but it hasn't been possible to remove the mountpoint before.

commit.subject: LU-28 fileset: add fileset mount support
commit.message:
LU-28 fileset: add fileset mount support

This patch enables client to mount subdirectory as fileset.

usage: mount -t lustre mgsname:/fsname/subdir /mount/point

* mdt lookup fileset fid and return to client during mount.
* `lfs fid2path` support for fileset.
